# Novada MCP

> Novada Hosted MCP is a remote Streamable-HTTP Model Context Protocol server at `https://mcp.novada.com/mcp` that exposes 15 web-data tools — web search, content extraction, site map, multi-page crawl, platform scraping, deep research, source/brand monitoring, cloud browser automation, and proxy credentials — through a single authenticated URL. Designed for AI agents running in Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Cline, Windsurf, or any MCP-compatible client. Zero install required; paste the URL and go.

## Endpoint

- URL: `https://mcp.novada.com/mcp`
- Auth (option A): query param `?token=YOUR_API_KEY`
- Auth (option B): header `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY` (preferred — keeps key out of logs and screen-shares)
- Auth (option C): path form `https://mcp.novada.com/YOUR_API_KEY/mcp`
- Transport: Streamable HTTP per MCP March 2025 spec
- Free gateway cap: 1,000 tool calls / month per API key — an anti-abuse cap on the free hosted gateway, independent of your Novada balance. No credit card required. Reaching it returns a JSON-RPC tool-error result (not HTTP 429); it resets at the start of next month (UTC), or use the local `npx novada-mcp` server which has no monthly gateway cap.
- Billing: pay-as-you-go on your Novada balance. Each call draws per-call usage at Novada's standard rates from the same balance used by the REST API and proxy. Some tools make multiple upstream calls and draw proportionally — e.g. `novada_research` reads several sources per call and `novada_crawl` fetches many pages per call.
- Rate limit: 60 requests / minute per source IP (exceeding it returns HTTP 429; not counted against the gateway cap)
- Infrastructure: Vercel Edge, 195+ countries, auto-SSL

## Tools

15 tools total, all callable on the hosted endpoint. (`novada_browser` runs on the hosted endpoint via cloud CDP; only the multi-step `novada_browser_flow` session tool is local-only — see Browser tools below.)

### Search & extraction (2)

- `novada_search`: web search via Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, or Yandex — returns titles, URLs, snippets
- `novada_extract`: read clean content from one or more URLs; handles anti-bot protection automatically (JS-render / anti-bot paths require the Web Unblocker product to be activated on your account); `format='html'` for raw HTML

### Site data (3)

- `novada_map`: list all URLs on a site via sitemap or crawl (URLs only, no content)
- `novada_crawl`: read content from multiple pages on one site (up to 20 pages per call)
- `novada_scrape`: structured data from platforms — Amazon, Walmart, Google, TikTok, LinkedIn, GitHub, YouTube, Twitter/X, and more (13 platforms, ~78 operations)

### Research & monitoring (4)

- `novada_research`: deep multi-source research — searches multiple angles, reads top sources, returns a synthesized report with citations (multiple upstream calls per invocation)
- `novada_ai_monitor`: search AI-company domains + the web for PUBLIC page mentions & sentiment of a brand. NOTE: searches indexed public pages — it does NOT query the live AI models
- `novada_monitor`: track changes on a web page over time (content hash + field-level diffs)
- `novada_discover`: list all available Novada tools grouped by category

### Browser (1)

- `novada_browser`: automate Novada's cloud browser via CDP — navigate, click, type, screenshot, snapshot. One-shot tasks per call. Runs on the hosted endpoint; credentials auto-provisioned from your API key.

### Proxy & account (5)

- `novada_proxy`: proxy credentials for your own HTTP clients — `type=residential|isp|datacenter|mobile|static|dedicated` (default residential). Returns credentials; does NOT auto-fetch. Not needed for extract/crawl (those handle proxies internally).
- `novada_account`: single-call account & billing dashboard — wallet balance, plan quotas, usage history, per-product plan balances, daily proxy traffic, health entitlements (`section=summary|balance|usage|plans|traffic`)
- `novada_setup`: check your API key and which Novada products are active (auth-free, no gateway cap used)
- `novada_proxy_account_list`: list proxy sub-accounts for a product (paginated)
- `novada_proxy_account_create`: create a proxy sub-account (two-step confirm gate)

## Install

### Claude Desktop (Option A — Custom Connector UI)

In Claude Desktop: Customize → Connectors → + → Add custom connector

URL to paste: `https://mcp.novada.com/mcp?token=YOUR_API_KEY`

### Claude Desktop (Option B — stdio bridge via mcp-remote)

Edit `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` (macOS) or `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json` (Windows):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "novada": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "mcp-remote", "https://mcp.novada.com/mcp?token=YOUR_API_KEY"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Claude Code

```bash
claude mcp add --transport http novada https://mcp.novada.com/mcp \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  --scope user
```

Verify with `claude mcp list` or `claude mcp get novada`.

### Cursor

Add to `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (or `.cursor/mcp.json` in project root for project scope):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "novada": {
      "url": "https://mcp.novada.com/mcp?token=YOUR_API_KEY"
    }
  }
}
```

### Codex

Add to `~/.codex/config.toml`:

```toml
[mcp_servers.novada]
url = "https://mcp.novada.com/mcp?token=YOUR_API_KEY"
```

To keep the key out of the file: set `bearer_token_env_var = "NOVADA_API_KEY"` and use `https://mcp.novada.com/mcp` as the URL.

### Cline

Edit `cline_mcp_settings.json` (Cline → MCP Servers → Edit):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "novada": {
      "type": "streamableHttp",
      "url": "https://mcp.novada.com/mcp?token=YOUR_API_KEY"
    }
  }
}
```

Note: `"type": "streamableHttp"` must be explicit; without it Cline defaults to SSE.

### Windsurf

Edit `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "novada": {
      "serverUrl": "https://mcp.novada.com/mcp?token=YOUR_API_KEY"
    }
  }
}
```

## Browser tools

`novada_browser` runs on the hosted endpoint — it drives Novada's cloud browser over CDP (navigate, click, type, screenshot, snapshot), one shot per call, with credentials auto-provisioned from your key.

`novada_browser_flow` (multi-step automation that holds a persistent session across calls) is NOT available on the hosted endpoint — Vercel serverless isolates cannot hold a persistent WebSocket session. Calling it on the hosted endpoint returns:

```
Error [NOT_AVAILABLE_ON_HOSTED]
agent_instruction: Use the local MCP server (`npx novada-mcp`) for browser-flow tasks, or use novada_scrape / novada_extract for static-content extraction on the hosted server.
```

To use `novada_browser_flow`, run the local MCP instead:

```bash
npx novada-mcp@latest
```

The same `YOUR_API_KEY` works. The local server also has no monthly gateway cap (per-call usage still draws your Novada balance).

## Source / repo

- GitHub: https://github.com/NovadaLabs/novada-mcpserver
- npm (local MCP): https://www.npmjs.com/package/novada-mcp (`npx novada-mcp@latest`)
- Get API key: https://dashboard.novada.com/api-key/
- Top up balance: https://dashboard.novada.com/

## FAQ

Full FAQ at https://mcp.novada.com/#faq. Key points:

- **Same key everywhere.** One Novada API key works for the hosted MCP, `npx novada-mcp` local, and the Novada REST API. Per-call usage draws the same Novada balance.
- **No separate MCP key needed.** Use your existing Novada API key.
- **Free gateway cap vs balance.** The 1,000-calls/month free-gateway cap is an anti-abuse throttle on the hosted endpoint, independent of billing. Per-call usage always draws your Novada balance; top up at dashboard.novada.com.
- **Hosted vs local.** Hosted = zero install, always latest, includes `novada_browser` (cloud CDP). Local (`npx novada-mcp`) = all 22 tools plus multi-step `novada_browser_flow`, no monthly gateway cap, you manage updates.
- **Key in URL vs header.** Both work identically. Prefer `Authorization: Bearer` header when storing configs in repos or sharing screens.
